title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
summary: Tool calling is a technique that enables AI agents to request external tools and services rather than relying solely on language model parameters. The video explains tool schema, system prompts that govern tool selection, and the agent-tool loop where models request tools and frameworks execute them.
writeup: This video from AWS Developers covers tool calling, the technique that lets AI agents request external tools and services instead of relying only on a language model's internal parameters. It walks through tool schemas, the system prompts that govern how a model selects tools, and the agent-tool loop in which the model requests a tool and the surrounding framework executes it. The practical takeaway: agents extend their capabilities by delegating to defined external tools, with the schema and system prompt shaping which tool gets chosen.
